title: viola macroceras
intro: Viola macroceras, also known as Japanese pimpernel, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Viola macroceras include its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to promote healing and reduce discomfort.
